211	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
212	
213	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
214	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
215	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
216	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
217	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
218	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
219	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
220	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
221	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
222	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
223	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/
224	
225	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
226	
227	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
228	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
229	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
230	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
231	
232	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
233	
234	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
235	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
236	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
237	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
238	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
239	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
240	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
241	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
242	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
243	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
244	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
245	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
246	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))
247	
248	/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
249	sheaf.**
250	
251	`tensorObj 𝒪_X 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_X`, where `𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
252	designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
253	`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
254	`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
255	sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
256	noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
257	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
258	      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
259	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
260	      (λ_ (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) ≪≫
261	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
262	      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
263	
264	/-- **Left unitor for `⊗_X`.** `𝒪_X ⊗_X M ≅ M`. (Blueprint
265	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, left half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor`.)
266	Sheafification of the presheaf-level left unitor `λ_ M.val`, composed with the
267	sheafification counit identifying `sheafification M.val` with the (already-sheaf)
268	`M`. The cheap `mapIso` pattern; uses no abstract pullback. -/
269	noncomputable def tensorObj_left_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
270	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) M ≅ M :=
271	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
272	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).leftUnitor M.val) ≪≫
273	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
274	
275	/-- **Right unitor for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X 𝒪_X ≅ M`. (Blueprint
276	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, right half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor`.)
277	Sheafification of the presheaf-level right unitor `ρ_ M.val`, composed with the
278	sheafification counit. -/
279	noncomputable def tensorObj_right_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
280	    tensorObj M (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅ M :=
281	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
282	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).rightUnitor M.val) ≪≫
283	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
284	
285	/-- **Braiding for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X N ≅ N ⊗_X M`. (Blueprint
286	`lem:tensorobj_comm_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_braiding`.)
287	The presheaf-of-modules monoidal category is symmetric; its braiding `β_ M.val
288	N.val` sheafifies to the asserted isomorphism by the cheap `mapIso` pattern. -/
289	noncomputable def tensorObj_braiding {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) :
290	    tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj N M :=
291	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
292	    (BraidedCategory.braiding
293	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) M.val N.val)
294	
295	/-- **Associator for `⊗_X` on `⊗`-invertible objects.** (Blueprint
296	`lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`.)
297	For `M, N, P` `⊗`-invertible (hence locally free of rank one, hence sectionwise
298	flat), there is an isomorphism `(M ⊗_X N) ⊗_X P ≅ M ⊗_X (N ⊗_X P)`. This is the
299	objectwise existence-of-iso datum the group law

380	  have e2 := a.mapIso
381	    ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).associator M.val N.val P.val)
382	  exact (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi1).symm ≪≫ e2 ≪≫ (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi3)
383	
384	/-- **Refining a trivialisation to a smaller open.** If `M` is trivialised on an
385	open `U` (`M.restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U`), it is trivialised on every open `W ≤ U`.
386	
387	The chart-chase is identical in spirit to `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`:
388	factor `W.ι = (X.homOfLE hWU) ≫ U.ι`, transport through `restrictFunctorCongr`
389	and `restrictFunctorComp` to identify `M.restrict W.ι` with
390	`(M.restrict U.ι).restrict (X.homOfLE hWU)`, restrict the given trivialisation
391	`e` along that open immersion, and identify the restricted unit with the unit
392	via `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (an isomorphism
393	because the open immersion's `Opens.map` is `Final`). -/
394	noncomputable def restrictIsoUnitOfLE {X : Scheme.{u}} {M : X.Modules} {U W : X.Opens}
395	    (hWU : W ≤ U)
396	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
397	    M.restrict W.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (W : Scheme).ringCatSheaf := by
398	  have hWU' : W ≤ (𝟙 X) ⁻¹ᵁ U := hWU
399	  set j : (W : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := Scheme.Hom.resLE (𝟙 X) U W hWU' with hj
400	  have hjι : j ≫ U.ι = W.ι := by rw [hj, Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι, Category.comp_id]
401	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map j.base).Final :=
402	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
403	  -- M.restrict W.ι ≅ (pullback W.ι).obj M
404	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.ι).app M ≪≫ ?_
405	  -- ≅ (pullback (j ≫ U.ι)).obj M
406	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hjι.symm).app M ≪≫ ?_
407	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
408	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp j U.ι).symm.app M ≪≫ ?_
409	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
410	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso
411	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M) ≪≫ ?_
412	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj 𝒪_U
413	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e ≪≫ ?_
414	  -- ≅ 𝒪_W
415	  haveI hI : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit j.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance
416	  exact @asIso _ _ _ _ _ hI
417	
418	/-- **Substrate tensor commutes with restriction along an open immersion.**
419	
420	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X` and `M N : X.Modules`,
421	`(tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f)`.
422	
423	This is the single substrate linchpin of `A.1.c.SubT` — **CLOSED, axiom-clean**
424	(iter-217). It says the substrate `⊗` (sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules
425	tensor) commutes with the restriction functor along an open immersion. The proof
426	is the blueprint's four-step composite:
427	  Step 1 (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`): reduce `restrict` to the abstract pullback.
428	  Step 2 (`SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`): move the pullback inside the
429	    sheafification (sheafification commutes with pullback).
430	  Step 3: strip the outer sheafification (`.mapIso`), descending to the presheaf goal
431	    `(pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val) ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`.
432	  Step 4: close that presheaf goal by **H1 ∘ H2**:
433	    • H1 (the sole Mathlib-ABSENT ingredient, BUILT this iter): the presheaf-level iso
434	      `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`, obtained from the de-sheafified presheaf
435	      `PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (adjunction along the open-immersion
436	      pair `f.opensFunctor ⊣ Opens.map f.base`) against the existing
437	      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq`. Here `β` is the
438	      `restrictFunctor` structure map, so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val`
439	      definitionally.
440	    • H2 (strong-monoidal tensorator): `pushforward β = pushforward₀ ⋙ restrictScalars β`
441	      with `β` sectionwise the open-immersion ring ISO `f.appIso`, so `restrictScalars β`
442	      is STRONG monoidal (`restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`, resting on the closed
443	      `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` / `restrictScalars_isIso_{μ,ε}`); the composite
444	      `μIso` is the tensorator.
445	The superseded `Localization.Monoidal` / `J.W.IsMonoidal` route is NOT used. -/
446	noncomputable def tensorObj_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
447	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M N : X.Modules) :
448	    (tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f) := by
449	  -- Step 1. Reduce `restrict` to `pullback` along the open immersion `f`
450	  -- (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Mathlib).
451	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (tensorObj M N) ≪≫ ?_
452	  -- Step 2. **Sheafification commutes with pullback.** `tensorObj M N` is, by
453	  -- definition, `sheafification.obj (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj
454	  -- M.val N.val)`, so the genuine Mathlib lemma
455	  -- `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`
456	  -- (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous`,
457	  -- `sheafification ⋙ pullback φ ≅ PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom ⋙
458	  -- sheafification`) moves the pullback *inside* the sheafification. This
459	  -- discharges half (ii) of the original obstruction (sheafification commuting
460	  -- with pullback). After it the goal is the purely **presheaf-level** residual
461	  -- `sheafify ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ N.val))
462	  --    ≅ (M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)`.
463	  refine (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app
464	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val) ≪≫ ?_
465	  -- Step 3. **Strip the outer sheafification.** Both sides are
466	  -- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)` applied to a
467	  -- presheaf-of-modules: the LHS to `(pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)`, and
468	  -- the RHS `(M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)` *by definition* to
469	  -- `(M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`. So it suffices to give the
470	  -- comparison at the PRESHEAF level and sheafify it. This is a genuine reduction
471	  -- step (verified: the goal below has no sheafification).
472	  refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_
473	  -- Step 4 (RESIDUAL CLOSURE — iter-217 H1 build). The remaining presheaf goal is
474	  --   `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)
475	  --      ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`
476	  -- where `φ = (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom` and `⊗ₚ =
477	  -- PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`. We close it via:
478	  --  (H1, the linchpin) the presheaf-level iso `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`, built from
479	  --      the presheaf `pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (above) against the existing
480	  --      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `leftAdjointUniq`. Here `β` is the
481	  --      open-immersion structure map of `restrictFunctor f`, so
482	  --      `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val` definitionally.
483	  --  (H2) the strong-monoidal comparison `(pushforward β).obj (A ⊗ₚ B) ≅
484	  --      (pushforward β).obj A ⊗ₚ (pushforward β).obj B`.
485	  -- `φR` (the scheme structure map) and `β` (the restrictFunctor structure map) are kept as
486	  -- `let`-bindings (zeta-transparent) so the unit/counit triangle goals below reduce; the
487	  -- open-immersion adjunction is INLINED for the same reason (a `have` would make `adj.unit`
488	  -- opaque and block the `congr` defeq, exactly as in Mathlib's sheaf-level `restrictAdjunction`).
489	  let φR := (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
490	  -- The restrictFunctor structure map `β` (so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val`).
491	  let α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
492	    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
493	  let β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
494	    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
495	  -- H1 via the presheaf pushforward-pushforward adjunction + `leftAdjointUniq`.
496	  have hadj : PresheafOfModules.pushforward β ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward φR :=
497	    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction β φR
498	      (by ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
499	      (by ext U x; exact congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))
500	  let H1 := hadj.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φR)
501	  refine (H1.app (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)).symm ≪≫ ?_
502	  -- H2: the strong-monoidal tensorator of `pushforward β = pushforward₀ ⋙ restrictScalars β`.
503	  -- `β` is sectionwise bijective (it is the `forget₂`-image of the open-immersion structure ring
504	  -- ISO `f.appIso`), so `restrictScalars β` is STRONG monoidal (`restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`),
505	  -- and `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat` is `Monoidal` (Mathlib); the composite's `μIso` is the tensorator.
506	  -- It is built over the SYNTACTIC `_ ⋙ forget₂` base form (where the `MonoidalCategory` instance is
507	  -- found canonically); the result is DEFEQ to the goal — whose base `X.ringCatSheaf.obj` is only
508	  -- defeq, not syntactically, `X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂` — and `(pushforward β).obj M.val =
509	  -- (M.restrict f).val` definitionally, so `exact` closes it without any instance diamond.
510	  have hβ : ∀ U, Function.Bijective (β.app U).hom := by
511	    intro U
512	    haveI : IsIso (β.app U) :=
513	      inferInstanceAs (IsIso ((forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map (f.appIso U.unop).inv))
514	    exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (β.app U)
515	  let β' : (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
516	      (f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat := β
517	  haveI : (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars β').Monoidal :=
518	    PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective β' hβ
519	  exact (Functor.Monoidal.μIso
520	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀OfCommRingCat f.opensFunctor X.presheaf
521	      ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars β')
522	    (M.val : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
523	    (N.val : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).symm
524	
525	/-- **Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial.**
526	
527	If `M, N : X.Modules` are locally trivial of rank one (line bundles), so is
528	their tensor product `tensorObj M N`. Per blueprint
529	`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`. The proof picks, for each point `x`,
530	a common affine open `W ∋ x` contained in trivialising opens `U` (for `M`) and
531	`U'` (for `N`), refines both trivialisations to `W` via `restrictIsoUnitOfLE`,
532	then transports through `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, the bifunctoriality
533	`tensorObjIsoOfIso`, and the unit isomorphism `tensorObj_unit_iso`:
534	`(M ⊗ N)|_W ≅ M|_W ⊗ N|_W ≅ 𝒪_W ⊗ 𝒪_W ≅ 𝒪_W`. The only residual gap is the
535	substrate-restriction compatibility `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
536	lemma tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
537	    (hM : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial M) (hN : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial N) :
538	    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (tensorObj M N) := by
539	  intro x
540	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, ⟨eM⟩⟩ := hM x
541	  obtain ⟨U', hxU', hU'_aff, ⟨eN⟩⟩ := hN x
542	  obtain ⟨W, hW_aff, hxW, hWsub⟩ :=
543	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := x) (U := U ⊓ U') ⟨hxU, hxU'⟩
544	  have hWU : W ≤ U := le_trans hWsub inf_le_left
545	  have hWU' : W ≤ U' := le_trans hWsub inf_le_right
546	  refine ⟨W, hxW, hW_aff, ⟨?_⟩⟩
547	  exact tensorObj_restrict_iso W.ι M N ≪≫
548	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU eM) (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU' eN) ≪≫
549	    tensorObj_unit_iso

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pullback
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafification
10	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PushforwardContinuous
11	
12	/-!
13	# Pullback of sheaves of modules
14	
15	Let `S` and `R` be sheaves of rings over sites `(C, J)` and `(D, K)` respectively.
16	Let `F : C ⥤ D` be a continuous functor between these sites, and
17	let `φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R` be a morphism
18	of sheaves of rings.
19	
20	In this file, we define the pullback functor for sheaves of modules
21	`pullback.{v} φ : SheafOfModules.{v} S ⥤ SheafOfModules.{v} R`
22	that is left adjoint to `pushforward.{v} φ`. We show that it exists
23	under suitable assumptions, and prove that the pullback of (pre)sheaves of
24	modules commutes with the sheafification.
25	
26	From the compatibility of `pushforward` with respect to composition, we deduce
27	similar pseudofunctor-like properties of the `pullback` functors.
28	
29	-/
30	
31	@[expose] public section
32	
33	universe v v₁ v₂ v₃ v₄ u₁ u₂ u₃ u₄ u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Functor
36	
37	namespace SheafOfModules
38	
39	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
40	  {D' : Type u₃} [Category.{v₃} D'] {D'' : Type u₄} [Category.{v₄} D'']
41	  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {F : C ⥤ D}
42	  {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
43	  [Functor.IsContinuous F J K]
44	  (φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R)
45	
46	section
47	
48	variable [(pushforward.{v} φ).IsRightAdjoint]
49	
50	/-- The pullback functor `SheafOfModules S ⥤ SheafOfModules R` induced by
51	a morphism of sheaves of rings `S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R`,
52	defined as the left adjoint functor to the pushforward, when it exists. -/
53	noncomputable def pullback : SheafOfModules.{v} S ⥤ SheafOfModules.{v} R :=
54	  (pushforward.{v} φ).leftAdjoint
55	
56	/-- Given a continuous functor between sites `F`, and a morphism of sheaves of rings
57	`S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R`, this is the adjunction
58	between the corresponding pullback and pushforward functors on the categories
59	of sheaves of modules. -/
60	noncomputable def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback.{v} φ ⊣ pushforward.{v} φ :=
61	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (pushforward φ)
62	
63	instance : (pullback.{v} φ).IsLeftAdjoint :=
64	  (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ).isLeftAdjoint
65	
66	end
67	
68	section
69	
70	variable [(PresheafOfModules.pushforward.{v} φ.hom).IsRightAdjoint]
71	  [HasWeakSheafify K AddCommGrpCat.{v}] [K.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v}]
72	
73	namespace PullbackConstruction
74	
75	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
76	/-- Construction of a left adjoint to the functor `pushforward.{v} φ` by using the
77	pullback of presheaves of modules and the sheafification. -/
78	noncomputable def adjunction :
79	    (forget S ⋙ PresheafOfModules.pullback.{v} φ.hom ⋙
80	      PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R₀ := R.obj) (𝟙 R.obj)) ⊣ pushforward.{v} φ :=
81	  Adjunction.mkOfHomEquiv
82	    { homEquiv := fun F G ↦
83	        ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 R.obj)).homEquiv _ _).trans
84	            (((PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ.hom).homEquiv F.val G.val).trans
85	              ((fullyFaithfulForget S).homEquiv (Y := (pushforward φ).obj G)).symm)
86	      homEquiv_naturality_left_symm := by
87	        intros
88	        dsimp [Functor.FullyFaithful.homEquiv]
89	        -- these erw seem difficult to remove
90	        erw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm,
91	          Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm]
92	        dsimp [pushforward_obj_val]
93	        simp only [Functor.map_comp, Category.assoc]
94	      homEquiv_naturality_right := by
95	        tauto }
96	
97	end PullbackConstruction
98	
99	instance : (pushforward.{v} φ).IsRightAdjoint :=
100	  (PullbackConstruction.adjunction.{v} φ).isRightAdjoint
101	
102	/-- The pullback functor on sheaves of modules can be described as a composition
103	of the forget functor to presheaves, the pullback on presheaves of modules, and
104	the sheafification functor. -/
105	noncomputable def pullbackIso :
106	    pullback.{v} φ ≅
107	      forget S ⋙ PresheafOfModules.pullback.{v} φ.hom ⋙
108	        PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R₀ := R.obj) (𝟙 R.obj) :=
109	  Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ)
110	    (PullbackConstruction.adjunction φ)
111	
112	section
113	
114	variable [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v}]
115	
116	/-- The pullback of (pre)sheaves of modules commutes with the sheafification. -/
117	noncomputable def sheafificationCompPullback :
118	    PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 S.obj) ⋙ pullback.{v} φ ≅
119	      PresheafOfModules.pullback.{v} φ.hom ⋙
120	        PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R₀ := R.obj) (𝟙 R.obj) :=
121	  Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq
122	    ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 S.obj)).comp
123	      (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ))
124	    ((PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ.hom).comp
125	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 R.obj)))
126	
127	end
128	
129	end
130	
131	
132	instance : (pushforward.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S)).IsRightAdjoint :=
133	  Functor.isRightAdjoint_of_iso (pushforwardId S).symm
134	
135	variable (S) in
136	/-- The pullback by the identity morphism identifies to the identity functor of the
137	category of sheaves of modules. -/
138	noncomputable def pullbackId : pullback.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
139	  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S))).leftAdjointIdIso (pushforwardId S)

224	def pushforwardCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pushforward f ≅ pushforward g :=
225	    pushforwardNatIso _ (Opens.mapIso _ _ (hf ▸ rfl)) ≪≫
226	      SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)

228	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
229	    ((pushforwardCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl

232	    ((pushforwardCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl

234	/-- Inverse images along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
235	def pullbackCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pullback f ≅ pullback g :=
236	  eqToIso (hf ▸ rfl)

Let me look at how `tensorObj_assoc_iso` is actually built and what whisker/sheafification-monoidality infrastructure exists.

300	`tensorObjIsoclassCommMonoid` consumes (associativity as a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)`).
301	
302	Status: CLOSED at the direct-`sorry` level (no `sorry` in this declaration's body);
303	it is `sorry`-transitive only through the route-(e) residual
304	`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. iter-212 go/no-go bridge CLEARED, the residual
305	located. The construction is the blueprint's three-step composite. Writing
306	`a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)`,
307	`η = sheafificationAdjunction.unit` (the localizer unit, `toPresheaf`-image
308	`toSheafify ∈ J.W`), and `α` the presheaf-of-modules associator:
309	  1. `a(η_{M.val ⊗ᵖ N.val} ▷ P.val)` is iso  (P flat ⇒ right-whiskered `η ∈ J.W`
310	     by `W_whiskerRight_of_flat`; `a` inverts `J.W` by `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W`),
311	     giving `(M ⊗ N) ⊗ P = a(a(M.val⊗N.val).val ⊗ P.val) ≅ a((M.val⊗N.val) ⊗ P.val)`;
312	  2. `a.mapIso α : a((M.val⊗N.val)⊗P.val) ≅ a(M.val⊗(N.val⊗P.val))`;
313	  3. `a(M.val ◁ η_{N.val ⊗ᵖ P.val})` is iso  (M flat), giving
314	     `a(M.val⊗(N.val⊗P.val)) ≅ a(M.val ⊗ a(N.val⊗P.val).val) = M ⊗ (N ⊗ P)`.
315	
316	**The go/no-go bridge `IsIso (a.map f)` from `J.W ((toPresheaf _).map f)` is now
317	CLOSED, axiom-clean**, as `PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` (it is
318	`PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms` — the
319	sheafification functor IS the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)` —
320	read at one morphism). The right-whisker conjugate
321	`PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_flat` is also closed axiom-clean.
322	
323	**The genuine residual is now the flatness feeding steps 1 and 3**: steps 1/3
324	need `J.W (toPresheaf (η ▷ P.val))` / `J.W (toPresheaf (M.val ◁ η))`, which
325	`W_whiskerLeft/Right_of_flat` supply ONLY from the SECTIONWISE flatness instance
326	`∀ U : (Opens X)ᵒᵖ, Module.Flat (𝒪_X(U)) (P.val(U))` (resp. M). This is NOT
327	derivable from `IsInvertible`: the only Mathlib route is single-ring
328	`Module.Invertible R m → Projective → Flat`, which would require `P.val(U)` to be
329	an invertible `𝒪_X(U)`-module for EVERY open `U` — false in general (the global
330	sections functor over a non-affine open is not exact and does not preserve
331	flatness; invertibility is a LOCAL/affine property). The blueprint's
332	"invertible ⇒ projective ⇒ flat" conflates global module-invertibility with
333	sectionwise-over-all-opens flatness. The mathematically-correct fix scopes
334	injectivity LOCALLY (local-triviality whiskering: on the cover where `P ≅ 𝒪`,
335	`η ▷ P ≅ η`, locally injective) — a new lemma needing `IsInvertible ⇒
336	IsLocallyTrivial` (blueprint-flagged nontrivial). A second obstacle is pure Lean
337	friction: `X.ringCatSheaf.val` is only defeq (not syntactically equal) to
338	`X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`, so the unit `η`'s monoidal
339	whiskering and the `Module.Flat` instance both fail typeclass synthesis / hit
340	heartbeat timeouts. See task result for the full corrected decomposition. -/
341	noncomputable def tensorObj_assoc_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N P : X.Modules} :
342	    tensorObj (tensorObj M N) P ≅ tensorObj M (tensorObj N P) := by
343	  -- UNCONDITIONAL (iter-238, step 0): the locally-trivial hypotheses are dropped —
344	  -- the body never consumed them (the whiskered-unit localizer fact holds for
345	  -- arbitrary modules under ROUTE (d)). Matches the blueprint `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`
346	  -- framed unconditional and enables `tensorObj_assoc_iso_invertible`.
347	  -- Bridge the monoidal structure across the `rfl`-defeq carrier
348	  -- `Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`.
349	  letI instMS : MonoidalCategoryStruct (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf)) :=
350	    inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategoryStruct
351	      (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
352	  set a := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) with ha
353	  -- Underlying presheaf tensors and the sheafification unit `η = toSheafify`.
354	  set MN := PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val with hMN
355	  set NP := PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) N.val P.val with hNP
356	  set η := (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
357	    (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).unit with hη
358	  -- The two whiskered-unit localizer facts (ROUTE (d), via `W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W`).
359	  -- `η_A = toSheafify` lies in `J.W` (`W_toSheafify`).
360	  have hηMN : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
361	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app MN)) := by
362	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
363	    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
364	  have hηNP : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
365	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app NP)) := by
366	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]

1	/-

4	Authors: Joël Riou
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Generator
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pushforward
10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Adjunction.PartialAdjoint
11	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Adjunction.CompositionIso
12	
13	/-!
14	# Pullback of presheaves of modules
15	
16	Let `F : C ⥤ D` be a functor, `R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat` and `S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat` be presheaves
17	of rings, and `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` be a morphism of presheaves of rings,
18	we introduce the pullback functor `pullback : PresheafOfModules S ⥤ PresheafOfModules R`
19	as the left adjoint of `pushforward : PresheafOfModules R ⥤ PresheafOfModules S`.
20	The existence of this left adjoint functor is obtained under suitable universe assumptions.
21	
22	From the compatibility of `pushforward` with respect to composition, we deduce
23	similar pseudofunctor-like properties of the `pullback` functors.
24	
25	-/
26	
27	@[expose] public section
28	
29	universe v v₁ v₂ v₃ v₄ u₁ u₂ u₃ u₄ u
30	
31	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite Functor
32	
33	namespace PresheafOfModules
34	
35	section
36	
37	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
38	  {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
39	  [(pushforward.{v} φ).IsRightAdjoint]
40	
41	/-- The pullback functor `PresheafOfModules S ⥤ PresheafOfModules R` induced by
42	a morphism of presheaves of rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, defined as the left adjoint
43	functor to the pushforward, when it exists. -/
44	noncomputable def pullback : PresheafOfModules.{v} S ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} R :=
45	  (pushforward.{v} φ).leftAdjoint
46	
47	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, this is the adjunction
48	between associated pullback and pushforward functors on the categories
49	of presheaves of modules. -/
50	noncomputable def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback.{v} φ ⊣ pushforward.{v} φ :=
51	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (pushforward φ)
52	
53	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, this is the property
54	that the (partial) left adjoint functor of `pushforward φ` is defined
55	on a certain object `M : PresheafOfModules S`. -/
56	abbrev pullbackObjIsDefined : ObjectProperty (PresheafOfModules.{v} S) :=
57	  (pushforward φ).leftAdjointObjIsDefined
58	
59	end
60

75	lemma pushforwardSections_unitHomEquiv
76	    {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (f : unit R ⟶ M) :
77	    pushforwardSections φ (M.unitHomEquiv f) =
78	      ((pushforward φ).obj M).unitHomEquiv
79	        (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ ≫ (pushforward φ).map f) := by
80	  ext X
81	  have := unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply φ (X := X) 1
82	  dsimp at this ⊢
83	  simp +instances [this, map_one]
84	  rfl
85	
86	variable [(pushforward.{u} φ).IsRightAdjoint]
87	
88	/-- The canonical morphism `(pullback.{u} φ).obj (unit S) ⟶ unit R`
89	of sheaves of modules corresponding to a continuous map between ringed sites. -/
90	noncomputable def pullbackObjUnitToUnit :
91	    (pullback.{u} φ).obj (unit S) ⟶ unit R :=
92	  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _).symm (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ)
93	
94	@[simp]
95	lemma pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_symm_unitToPushforwardObjUnit :
96	    ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _).symm (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ) =
97	      pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ := rfl
98	
99	@[simp]
100	lemma pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit :
101	    (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _ (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ) =
102	      unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ :=
103	  Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _
104	
105	instance [F.Final] : IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ) := by
106	  rw [isIso_iff_coyoneda_map_bijective]
107	  intro M
108	  rw [← ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _).bijective.of_comp_iff',
109	    ← (unitHomEquiv _).bijective.of_comp_iff']
110	  convert (bijective_pushforwardSections φ M).comp (unitHomEquiv _).bijective
111	  ext f : 1
112	  dsimp
113	  rw [pushforwardSections_unitHomEquiv, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq,
114	    Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right,
115	    pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit]
116	
117	variable [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [HasWeakSheafify K AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
118	  [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
119	  [K.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [F.Final]
120	
121	/-- The pullback of a free sheaf of modules is a free sheaf of modules. -/
122	noncomputable def pullbackObjFreeIso (I : Type u) :
123	    (pullback φ).obj (free I) ≅ free I :=
124	  (asIso (sigmaComparison _ _)).symm ≪≫
125	    Sigma.mapIso (fun _ ↦ asIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ))
126	
127	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
128	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
129	lemma pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom {I : Type u} (i : I) :
130	    (pullback φ).map (ιFree i) ≫ (pullbackObjFreeIso φ I).hom =
131	      pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ ≫ ιFree i := by
132	  simp [pullbackObjFreeIso, ιFree]
133	
134	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
135	lemma pullbackObjFreeIso_hom_naturality {I J : Type u} (f : I → J) :
136	    (pullback φ).map (freeMap f) ≫ (pullbackObjFreeIso φ J).hom =
137	      (pullbackObjFreeIso φ I).hom ≫ freeMap f :=
138	  Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (isColimitCofanMkObjOfIsColimit (pullback φ) _ _
139	    (isColimitFreeCofan (R := S) I)) _ _ (fun i ↦ by simp [← Functor.map_comp_assoc])
140	
141	/-- The canonical isomorphism `freeFunctor ⋙ pullback φ ≅ freeFunctor` for a
142	continuous map between ringed sites, when the underlying functor between the sites
143	is final. -/
144	noncomputable def freeFunctorCompPullbackIso : freeFunctor ⋙ pullback φ ≅ freeFunctor :=
145	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun X ↦ pullbackObjFreeIso φ X)
146	
147	end SheafOfModules

904	      (adj.counit.app X ⊗ₘ adj.counit.app X') ≫ _ by rw [this]
905	    simpa using NatTrans.whiskerRight_app_tensor_app adj.counit adj.counit (f := f) X'
906	  μ_natural_right {X' Y'} X g := by
907	    simp only [Adjunction.homEquiv_apply, ← adj.unit_naturality_assoc, ← G.map_comp,
908	      assoc, ← δ_natural_right_assoc F]
909	    suffices F.obj (G.obj X) ◁ F.map (G.map g) ≫ _ =

911	    simpa using NatTrans.whiskerLeft_app_tensor_app adj.counit adj.counit (f := g) _
912	  associativity X Y Z := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm.injective (by

918	    dsimp only [id_obj, comp_obj, Functor.comp_map, Functor.id_map] at this
919	    rw [this, this, tensorHom_def, assoc, ← comp_whiskerRight_assoc,

921	      whisker_exchange_assoc, whisker_exchange_assoc, ← tensorHom_def_assoc,

941	      homEquiv_counit, map_comp, map_comp, map_comp, map_comp, map_comp, map_comp,

954	  leftAdjoint_μ (X Y : D) : μ G X Y =

975	  simp [IsMonoidal.leftAdjoint_μ (adj := adj), ← adj.unit_naturality_assoc,
976	    ← Functor.map_comp, ← δ_natural_assoc]

985	      δ F _ _ ≫ (adj.counit.app X ⊗ₘ adj.counit.app Y) := by
986	  simp [IsMonoidal.leftAdjoint_μ (adj := adj)]
987	
988	instance : (Adjunction.id (C := C)).IsMonoidal where

991	    [F'.OplaxMonoidal] [G'.LaxMonoidal] [adj'.IsMonoidal] : (adj.comp adj').IsMonoidal where
992	  leftAdjoint_ε := by
993	    simp [IsMonoidal.leftAdjoint_ε (adj := adj'), IsMonoidal.leftAdjoint_ε (adj := adj),
994	      ← map_comp, ← adj'.unit_naturality_assoc]
995	  leftAdjoint_μ X Y := by
996	    simp only [comp_obj, comp_μ, IsMonoidal.leftAdjoint_μ (adj := adj), id_obj,
997	      IsMonoidal.leftAdjoint_μ (adj := adj'), assoc, ← map_comp, comp_unit_app, comp_δ,
998	      comp_counit_app, ← tensorHom_comp_tensorHom, δ_natural_assoc, Functor.comp_map]

1004	variable [G.LaxMonoidal]

1007	/-- The left adjoint of a lax monoidal functor is oplax monoidal. -/
1008	@[simps -isSimp, implicit_reducible]
1009	def leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal : F.OplaxMonoidal where
1010	  η := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm (ε G)
1011	  δ X Y := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm ((adj.unit.app X ⊗ₘ adj.unit.app Y) ≫ μ G _ _)
1012	  δ_natural_left _ _ := by
1013	    rw [← Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right_symm,
1014	      ← Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm, assoc, ← μ_natural_left]
1015	    simp [← tensorHom_id]
1016	  δ_natural_right _ _ := by
1017	    rw [← Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right_symm,
1018	      ← Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm, assoc, ← μ_natural_right]
1019	    simp [← id_tensorHom]
1020	  oplax_associativity X Y Z := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by
1021	    rw [← Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right_symm,
1022	      ← Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right_symm,
1023	      ← Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm,
1024	      Equiv.apply_symm_apply, Equiv.apply_symm_apply, assoc, assoc]
1025	    conv_lhs =>
1026	      rw [homEquiv_counit, map_comp_assoc, map_comp,
1027	        ← μ_natural_left_assoc, map_comp, map_comp, tensorHom_def'_assoc]
1028	      dsimp
1029	      rw [← comp_whiskerRight_assoc]
1030	    conv_rhs =>
1031	      rw [← μ_natural_right, homEquiv_counit, map_comp_assoc,
1032	        map_comp, tensorHom_def_assoc, ← associator_naturality_left_assoc]

1036	        unit_naturality_assoc, right_triangle_components, comp_id, assoc,

1038	        ← associator_naturality_middle_assoc, ← associator_naturality_right_assoc,

1041	    simp)

1043	    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right,
1044	      Equiv.apply_symm_apply, assoc, ← μ_natural_left, ← tensorHom_id,
1045	      tensorHom_comp_tensorHom_assoc]
1046	    simp [tensorHom_def', homEquiv_unit, homEquiv_counit])
1047	  oplax_right_unitality _ := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by
1048	    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right,
1049	      Equiv.apply_symm_apply, assoc, ← μ_natural_right, ← id_tensorHom,
1050	      tensorHom_comp_tensorHom_assoc]
1051	    simp [tensorHom_def, homEquiv_unit, homEquiv_counit])
1052	
1053	instance :
1054	    letI := adj.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal
1055	    adj.IsMonoidal := by
1056	  letI := adj.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal
1057	  refine ⟨?_, fun X Y ↦ ?_⟩
1058	  · simp [homEquiv_counit, leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal_η]

730	/-! ## §5. The invertibility-carrier Picard group `picCommGroup`
731	
732	This is the by-hand commutative-group law on isomorphism classes of `⊗`-invertible
733	`𝒪_X`-modules (blueprint §`sec:tensorobj_pic_carrier`). Every group axiom is a single
734	existence-of-isomorphism `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` read as an equality of iso-classes; no
735	pentagon/triangle/hexagon coherence and no `MonoidalCategory` instance is invoked.
736	The inverse is carried by the membership witness of `IsInvertible` itself. -/
737	
738	/-- **Step 1 — associator on `⊗`-invertible objects** (blueprint
739	`lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso_invertible`). An immediate specialisation of the now
740	*unconditional* `tensorObj_assoc_iso`; the invertibility hypotheses are not consumed
741	(they match the blueprint statement). -/
742	noncomputable def tensorObj_assoc_iso_invertible {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N P : X.Modules}
743	    (_hM : IsInvertible M) (_hN : IsInvertible N) (_hP : IsInvertible P) :
744	    tensorObj (tensorObj M N) P ≅ tensorObj M (tensorObj N P) :=
745	  tensorObj_assoc_iso
746	
747	/-- **Middle-four interchange for `⊗_X`** (helper). For arbitrary `𝒪_X`-modules
748	`A, B, C, D`, there is an isomorphism `(A ⊗ B) ⊗ (C ⊗ D) ≅ (A ⊗ C) ⊗ (B ⊗ D)`,
749	assembled from the unconditional associator and the braiding (no coherence consumed).
750	Used to reassociate the four factors in `IsInvertible.tensorObj`. -/
751	private noncomputable def tensorObj_middleFour {X : Scheme.{u}} (A B C D : X.Modules) :
752	    tensorObj (tensorObj A B) (tensorObj C D)
753	      ≅ tensorObj (tensorObj A C) (tensorObj B D) :=
754	  tensorObj_assoc_iso ≪≫
755	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl A)
756	      (tensorObj_assoc_iso.symm ≪≫
757	        tensorObjIsoOfIso (tensorObj_braiding B C) (Iso.refl D) ≪≫
758	        tensorObj_assoc_iso) ≪≫
759	    tensorObj_assoc_iso.symm
760	
761	/-- **Step 3 — `⊗`-invertibility is closed under tensor product** (blueprint
762	`lem:isinvertible_tensor`). If `M, M'` are `⊗`-invertible with inverses `N, N'`,
763	then `N ⊗ N'` is a tensor inverse of `M ⊗ M'`. -/
764	theorem IsInvertible.tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' : X.Modules}
765	    (hM : IsInvertible M) (hM' : IsInvertible M') :
766	    IsInvertible (Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M M') := by
767	  obtain ⟨N, ⟨e⟩⟩ := hM
768	  obtain ⟨N', ⟨e'⟩⟩ := hM'
769	  exact ⟨Scheme.Modules.tensorObj N N',
770	    ⟨tensorObj_middleFour M M' N N' ≪≫ tensorObjIsoOfIso e e' ≪≫ tensorObj_unit_iso⟩⟩
771	
772	/-- **Step 4 — the structure sheaf is `⊗`-invertible** (blueprint
773	`lem:isinvertible_unit`). Witness `𝒪_X`, iso `tensorObj_unit_iso`. -/
774	theorem isInvertible_unit {X : Scheme.{u}} :
775	    IsInvertible (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :=
776	  ⟨SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf, ⟨tensorObj_unit_iso⟩⟩
777	
778	/-- **Step 5 — the tensor inverse is determined up to isomorphism** (blueprint
779	`lem:isinvertible_inverse_welldef`). If `M ⊗ N ≅ 𝒪_X` and `M ⊗ N' ≅ 𝒪_X` then
780	`N ≅ N'`, via the inverse-of-inverse chain. -/
781	theorem IsInvertible.inverse_unique {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N N' : X.Modules}
782	    (e : Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
783	    (e' : Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M N' ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :
784	    Nonempty (N ≅ N') :=
785	  ⟨(tensorObj_right_unitor N).symm ≪≫
786	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl N) e'.symm ≪≫
787	    tensorObj_assoc_iso.symm ≪≫
788	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (tensorObj_braiding N M ≪≫ e) (Iso.refl N') ≪≫
789	    tensorObj_left_unitor N'⟩
790	
791	/-- The setoid of `⊗`-invertible `𝒪_X`-modules: `M ∼ M'` iff there exists an
792	isomorphism `M ≅ M'` (blueprint `def:pic_carrier`). -/
793	instance picSetoid (X : Scheme.{u}) : Setoid {M : X.Modules // IsInvertible M} where
794	  r M M' := Nonempty ((M : X.Modules) ≅ (M' : X.Modules))
795	  iseqv :=
796	    ⟨fun _ => ⟨Iso.refl _⟩, fun ⟨e⟩ => ⟨e.symm⟩, fun ⟨e⟩ ⟨f⟩ => ⟨e ≪≫ f⟩⟩
797	
798	/-- **Step 2 — the invertibility-carrier Picard group** (blueprint
799	`def:pic_carrier`): the quotient of `⊗`-invertible `𝒪_X`-modules by isomorphism. -/
800	def PicGroup (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type _ := Quotient (picSetoid X)
801	
802	/-- Multiplication on `PicGroup X`: `[M] · [M'] := [M ⊗_X M']`, well-defined on
803	iso-classes by bifunctoriality (`tensorObjIsoOfIso`), landing in `PicGroup` by
804	`IsInvertible.tensorObj`. -/
805	noncomputable def picMul {X : Scheme.{u}} : PicGroup X → PicGroup X → PicGroup X :=
806	  Quotient.lift₂
807	    (fun a b => Quotient.mk _ ⟨tensorObj a.1 b.1, a.2.tensorObj b.2⟩)
808	    (by
809	      rintro ⟨a, ha⟩ ⟨b, hb⟩ ⟨a', ha'⟩ ⟨b', hb'⟩ ⟨ea⟩ ⟨eb⟩
810	      exact Quotient.sound ⟨tensorObjIsoOfIso ea eb⟩)
811	
812	/-- The inverse class on `PicGroup X`: `[M] ↦ [N]` for the membership witness `N`
813	of `IsInvertible M`, well-defined by `IsInvertible.inverse_unique`. -/
814	noncomputable def picInv {X : Scheme.{u}} : PicGroup X → PicGroup X :=
815	  Quotient.lift
816	    (fun a => Quotient.mk _
817	      ⟨Classical.choose a.2,
818	        a.1, ⟨tensorObj_braiding _ a.1 ≪≫ (Classical.choose_spec a.2).some⟩⟩)
819	    (by
820	      rintro ⟨a, ha⟩ ⟨a', ha'⟩ ⟨ea⟩
821	      refine Quotient.sound ?_
822	      -- both `Classical.choose ha` and `Classical.choose ha'` are tensor inverses of `a`
823	      have h1 : tensorObj a (Classical.choose ha) ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
824	        (Classical.choose_spec ha).some
825	      have h2 : tensorObj a (Classical.choose ha') ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
826	        tensorObjIsoOfIso ea (Iso.refl _) ≪≫ (Classical.choose_spec ha').some
827	      exact IsInvertible.inverse_unique h1 h2)
828	
829	/-- **Step 6 — the invertibility-carrier Picard group is abelian** (blueprint
830	`thm:pic_commgroup`). `[M] · [M'] := [M ⊗_X M']`, `1 := [𝒪_X]`, and `[M]⁻¹` the
831	class of any membership witness of `IsInvertible M`. Each group axiom is a single
832	existence-of-isomorphism: unit laws ← unitors, associativity ← associator,
833	commutativity ← braiding, left inverse ← the witness iso. No monoidal coherence. -/
834	noncomputable instance picCommGroup (X : Scheme.{u}) : CommGroup (PicGroup X) where
835	  mul := picMul
836	  one := Quotient.mk _ ⟨SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf, isInvertible_unit⟩
837	  inv := picInv
838	  mul_assoc := by
839	    rintro a b c
840	    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
841	    induction b using Quotient.ind with | _ b => ?_
842	    induction c using Quotient.ind with | _ c => ?_
843	    exact Quotient.sound ⟨tensorObj_assoc_iso⟩
844	  one_mul := by
845	    rintro a

/-! ## §6. Pullback-monoidality substrate (A.1.c): `IsInvertible.pullback`

Project-local Mathlib supplement. The relative Picard consumer re-bases onto the
`IsInvertible` carrier and its structure maps are *pullback* maps for GENERAL scheme
morphisms (the projection `C ×_S T → T` and base-change maps are neither open
immersions nor flat). We need that pullback preserves `⊗`-invertibility. This requires
`pullbackTensorIso` (`f^*(M ⊗ N) ≅ f^*M ⊗ f^*N`) and `pullbackUnitIso`
(`f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y`). Blueprint §`sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`. -/

/-- **Sheafification reconciles the presheaf tensor with the tensor of the
sheafified factors.** For presheaves of modules `P, Q` on `X`, sheafifying the
presheaf tensor `P ⊗ Q` agrees with sheafifying the tensor of the underlying
presheaves of their sheafifications `(a P).val ⊗ (a Q).val`, where
`a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 𝒪_X)`. This is the "sheafification is
monoidal" reconciliation, built — exactly as in `tensorObj_assoc_iso` — by
whiskering the sheafification unit `η` (a `J.W`-morphism, hence locally bijective)
on each side and inverting under `a` via `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` together
with the flatness-free `W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W`. It is the bridge reconciling a
presheaf-level tensorator with the substrate `⊗_X` (whose `.val` carries an extra
sheafification on each factor), as needed by the pullback-monoidality comparison
`pullbackTensorIso`. -/
private noncomputable def sheafifyTensorUnitIso {X : Scheme.{u}}
    (P Q : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
        (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj P Q) ≅
      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
        (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj
          ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification
              (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj P).val
          ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification
              (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj Q).val) := by
  letI instMS : MonoidalCategoryStruct (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf)) :=
    inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategoryStruct
      (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
  set a := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) with ha
  set η := (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
    (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).unit with hη
  have hηP : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app P)) := by
    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
  have hηQ : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app Q)) := by
    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
  have hW1 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app P ▷ Q)) :=
    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_W (R := X.presheaf) Q (η.app P) hηP
  have hW2 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (_ ◁ η.app Q)) :=
    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_W (R := X.presheaf) _ (η.app Q) hηQ
  have hi1 : IsIso (a.map (η.app P ▷ Q)) :=
    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW1
  have hi2 : IsIso (a.map (_ ◁ η.app Q)) :=
    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW2
  exact (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi1) ≪≫ (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi2)

end Modules

input.command: cd /home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; timeout 300 python3 .claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py --provider auto "In Lean4 Mathlib (current), I need to prove: for a morphism of schemes f : X ⟶ Y, the pullback functor on O-modules satisfies f^*(M ⊗ N) ≅ f^*M ⊗ f^*N (Stacks lemma-tensor-product-pullback). In Mathlib, SheafOfModules.pullback φ and PresheafOfModules.pullback φ are defined ABSTRACTLY as (pushforward φ).leftAdjoint — there is NO sectionwise formula and NO stalkwise formula available. The tensor product on sheaves is built as sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor. Mathlib has: Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal (left adjoint of a lax monoidal functor is oplax monoidal, giving a comparison map δ : F(A⊗B) ⟶ FA ⊗ FB for free), and Functor.Monoidal.ofOplaxMonoidal (upgrades oplax to strong if η and δ are isos). The pushforward = pushforward0 ∘ restrictScalars is lax monoidal. The PROBLEM: proving IsIso δ requires knowing the abstract left adjoint pullback acts sectionwise as extendScalars, which has no Mathlib API. QUESTION: What is the cleanest abstract/categorical way to prove δ (the oplax comparison of the pullback) is an isomorphism, WITHOUT a sectionwise formula? Is there a 'projection formula' or 'monoidal adjunction implies strong left adjoint' criterion? Be concrete about category-theory lemmas." 2>&1 | tail -50

---def IsLocallyTrivial location---
AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundlePullback.lean

150	`M|_U` becomes a trivialising chart for `(f^* M)|_(f⁻¹ U)`. The full chart-chase
151	involves the natural-isomorphism `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` plus the
152	preservation of `SheafOfModules.unit` under pullback (Mathlib
153	`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackFree`'s
154	`unitToPushforwardObjUnit`). Iter-187 wraps this chase as a named typed sorry
155	(helper-budget = 1); iter-188+ instantiates the chart-chase. -/
156	lemma IsLocallyTrivial.pullback {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) {M : X.Modules}
157	    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
158	    IsLocallyTrivial ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M) := by
159	  intro y
160	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, eM⟩ := hM (f.base y)
161	  have hyU' : y ∈ f ⁻¹ᵁ U := hxU
162	  obtain ⟨V, hV_aff, hyV, hVU⟩ := exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset hyU'
163	  refine ⟨V, hyV, hV_aff, ?_⟩
164	  -- Stacks 01HH chart-chase: factor `V.ι ≫ f` through `U` using
165	  -- `Scheme.Hom.resLE`, then transport through `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`
166	  -- and `pullbackComp` to identify the pullback at `V` with the pullback of
167	  -- the unit on `U` to `V`. Close via `eM` and `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`
168	  -- (which is an iso since `Opens.map g.base` is final via the
169	  -- `RepresentablyFlat` instance).
170	  obtain ⟨eM⟩ := eM
171	  set g : (V : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := f.resLE U V hVU with hg_def
172	  have hg_comp : g ≫ U.ι = V.ι ≫ f := Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι f hVU
173	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final :=
174	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
175	  refine ⟨?_⟩
176	  -- 1: ((pullback f).obj M).restrict V.ι ≅ (pullback V.ι).obj ((pullback f).obj M)
177	  let i1 :=
178	    (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback V.ι).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M)
179	  -- 2: ≅ (pullback (V.ι ≫ f)).obj M
180	  let i2 := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp V.ι f).app M
181	  -- 3: ≅ (pullback (g ≫ U.ι)).obj M  (using V.ι ≫ f = g ≫ U.ι)
182	  let i3 := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hg_comp.symm).app M
183	  -- 4: ≅ (pullback g).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
184	  let i4 := ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g U.ι).symm).app M
185	  -- 5: ≅ (pullback g).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
186	  let i5 := (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapIso
187	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M)
188	  -- 6: ≅ (pullback g).obj (unit_U)
189	  let i6 := (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapIso eM
190	  -- 7: ≅ unit_V  (pullback of unit is unit via the Mathlib morphism, which is
191	  -- iso since the underlying `Opens.map g.base` is Final)
192	  let i7 := asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom)
193	  exact i1 ≪≫ i2 ≪≫ i3 ≪≫ i4 ≪≫ i5 ≪≫ i6 ≪≫ i7
194

This is a key realization: `IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` is **fully proven for general morphisms** via a local-chart trick (factoring through a local map `g` whose `Opens.map g.base` IS Final, making `pullbackObjUnitToUnit g` an iso). This same trick may unblock `pullbackUnitIso`. Let me check `isIso_of_isIso_restrict`.

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the d.2-free descent re-route (B-connector)

The "locally-iso ⇒ iso" half of the descent assembly of `exists_tensorObj_inverse`:
a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on an open
neighbourhood of every point is a global isomorphism. The route is the stalkwise
iso criterion `TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (for sheaves valued
in `Ab`, whose forgetful functor reflects isos and preserves limits / filtered
colimits) together with `Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso` (restriction along an
open immersion commutes with stalks). **No stalk-⊗ ("d.2") is invoked**: this is a
statement about a single module morphism, never about the tensor stalk. -/

/-- **B-connector: a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on
an open cover is an isomorphism.** For `φ : M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules`, if every point
`x` lies in an open `U x` on which the restriction `(restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ`
is an isomorphism, then `φ` is an isomorphism. This is the B-bridge of the d.2-free
descent re-route assembling `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
lemma isIso_of_isIso_restrict {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N)
    (U : X → X.Opens) (hxU : ∀ x, x ∈ U x)
    (h : ∀ x, IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ)) :
    IsIso φ := by
  -- It suffices that each stalk map of the underlying `Ab`-sheaf morphism is iso.
  have hst : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map
      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) := by
    intro x
    obtain ⟨x', hx'⟩ : ∃ x', (U x).ι x' = x := by
      have hmem : x ∈ (U x).ι.opensRange := by
        rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]; exact hxU x
      exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.mem_opensRange.mp hmem
    haveI : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ) := h x
    -- `(restrictFunctor … ⋙ toPresheaf … ⋙ stalkFunctor x').map φ` is iso (functor of an iso).
    haveI hFφ : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι ⋙
        Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x').map φ) := by
      dsimp only [Functor.comp_map]; exact Functor.map_isIso _ _
    -- Transport the iso across `restrictStalkNatIso` to the stalk at `(U x).ι x' = x`.
    have hGφ : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} ((U x).ι x')).map
        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) :=
      (CategoryTheory.NatIso.isIso_map_iff
        (Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso (U x).ι x') φ).mp hFφ
    exact hx' ▸ hGφ
  -- Package as a morphism of `TopCat.Sheaf Ab X` and apply the stalkwise iso criterion.
  let MS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩
  let NS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨N.presheaf, N.isSheaf⟩
  let fS : MS ⟶ NS := ⟨(Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ⟩
  haveI : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map fS.hom) := hst
  haveI hSiso : IsIso fS := TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso fS
  have h1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ) := by
    have := (TopCat.Sheaf.forget Ab.{u} X).map_isIso fS
    exact this
  exact (CategoryTheory.isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso φ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X)).mp h1

/-- **A-bridge step (ii): promote an `𝒪_X`-linear `Ab`-presheaf morphism to a module
morphism.** Given a morphism `g : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf` of the underlying
`Ab`-presheaves that is sectionwise `𝒪_X`-linear, package it as a morphism `M ⟶ N`
of `𝒪_X`-modules. This wraps `PresheafOfModules.homMk` at the `Scheme.Modules` level;
it is the "promote to `𝒪_X`-linear" half of the descent A-bridge `homOfLocalCompat`
(the ab-sheaf gluing produces the linear `g`; this lemma turns it into a module map).
Sectionwise linearity is a property the consumer checks on a separated presheaf. -/
noncomputable def homMk {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
    (g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)
    (hg : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V) (m : M.val.obj V),
      (g.app V).hom (r • m) = r • (g.app V).hom m) :
    M ⟶ N :=
  ⟨PresheafOfModules.homMk (M₁ := M.val) (M₂ := N.val) g hg⟩

/-- The underlying `Ab`-presheaf morphism of `homMk g hg` is `g` itself. -/
@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_map_homMk {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
    (g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)
    (hg : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V) (m : M.val.obj V),
      (g.app V).hom (r • m) = r • (g.app V).hom m) :
    (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map (homMk g hg) = g :=
  rfl

The PRIMARY `dual_isLocallyTrivial` reduces, exactly as `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`

(`(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val` definitionally, `change`-confirmed).

**Precise decomposition of what (ii) actually needs** (the genuine new build; the

natural in `V` and `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear, induced by the slice equivalence

`f.opensFunctor` is fully faithful with image `= {W ≤ U}` and `fV ≤ U`), TOGETHER WITH
the identification `restr (fV) A ≅ G^* (restr V (pushforward β A))` under that

/-- **Pullback preserves the structure sheaf** (blueprint `lem:pullback_unit_iso`):
for a general scheme morphism `f : Y ⟶ X`, `f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y`. The canonical Mathlib
comparison `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an isomorphism only when the
underlying site functor is `Final` (true for open immersions, false in general); we
upgrade it to an iso for arbitrary `f` by the local-chart-finality trick already used
in `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`: on each affine chart `V` the restriction
factors through the local map `g = f.resLE …` whose `Opens.map g.base` IS final, so
`pullbackObjUnitToUnit g` is an iso there; `isIso_of_isIso_restrict` then promotes the
global comparison to an isomorphism. -/
noncomputable def pullbackUnitIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅
      SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf := by
  have hiso : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) := by
    refine isIso_of_isIso_restrict (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)
      (fun y => ?_) (fun y => ?_) (fun y => ?_)
    · exact (exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := Y) (x := y) (U := ⊤) trivial).choose
    · exact (exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := Y) (x := y) (U := ⊤) trivial).choose_spec.2.1
    · sorry
  exact @asIso _ _ _ _ _ hiso

end Modules

/- **HANDOFF — `pullbackUnitIso` / `pullbackTensorIso` / `IsInvertible.pullback`
(blueprint `sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`): NOT closable this iter; the planner's
recipe is structurally blocked, and a concrete pivot route is identified.**

The wall (verified live this iter):
  • `Scheme.Modules.pullback f = SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom` and the
    underlying `PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom` are BOTH defined as
    `(pushforward _).leftAdjoint` — an ABSTRACT left adjoint with NO sectionwise and NO
    stalkwise formula in Mathlib at the pinned commit. (`grep` confirms: no
    `pullback_obj` / `pullbackObjIso` / monoidal-`pullback` anywhere in
    `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/{Presheaf,Sheaf}/`.)
  • Hence the plan-agent recipe — "the presheaf pullback is strong monoidal sectionwise
    via `(extendScalars f).Monoidal`; assemble the sectionwise tensorators `μ`" — cannot
    typecheck: there is no sectionwise `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).obj` to attach the
    `extendScalars` tensorator to.
  • `Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal` DOES give a comparison MAP `δ : f^*(A⊗B) ⟶
    f^*A ⊗ f^*B` for free (from `pushforward` lax-monoidal), but only at the PRESHEAF
    level (no `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules …)` exists). Proving `IsIso δ` still
    reduces, via `isIso_of_isIso_app`, to a SECTIONWISE identification of the abstract
    pullback with `extendScalars` — i.e. the same missing formula. Invertibility of `δ`
    is the genuine content (extension of scalars is strong; restriction is only lax), not
    derivable purely abstractly.
  • `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an iso only under `F.Final` (open
    immersions), false for general `f`.

RECOMMENDED PIVOT (do NOT re-dispatch the sectionwise-`extendScalars` recipe):
  (1) **Local-chart-finality route** (the trick that ALREADY proves
      `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` for GENERAL `f`, `LineBundlePullback.lean`
      L156): an iso of `𝒪`-modules is checked LOCALLY via the axiom-clean
      `isIso_of_isIso_restrict` (this file, L567). On each affine chart `V ∋ y` the
      relevant map factors through the LOCAL map `g = f.resLE U V` whose `Opens.map g.base`
      IS `Final` (`final_of_representablyFlat`), so `pullbackObjUnitToUnit g` is an iso.
      For `pullbackUnitIso` this reduces (probed live) to
        `IsIso ((restrictFunctor V.ι).map (pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom))`,
      whose closure needs Mathlib-absent naturality of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` against
      `pullbackComp` / `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` (a small lemma cluster — the genuine
      next sub-step). `pullbackTensorIso` is harder: it has NO canonical comparison map at
      the sheaf level, so the oplax `δ` must first be transported to the sheaf level (or a
      stalkwise comparison built), then shown locally iso by the same trick.
  (2) **Sheafification reconciliation is already landed**: `sheafifyTensorUnitIso` (this
      file, just above) is the RHS-reconciliation brick the eventual `pullbackTensorIso`
      consumes — it bridges `a(P ⊗ₚ Q)` with `a((a P).val ⊗ₚ (a Q).val)` (sheafification
      is monoidal up to the unit), via the SAME `W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W` +
      `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` technique that closes the associator.
  (3) **STRATEGIC alternative for the consumer**: the RPF structure maps are the
      projection `π_T : C ×_S T → T` (FLAT, since `C → S = Spec k` is flat) and base
      changes thereof (also flat). A FLAT-restricted `IsInvertible.pullback`, or carrying
      pullback functoriality on the already-general `IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` and
      bridging to `IsInvertible` only at the group law, may avoid the general
      pullback-monoidal build entirely. Worth a strategy-critic / mathlib-analogist pass
      before committing to (1). (Informal agent unavailable this iter: MOONSHOT key 401,
      no other key set.)
-/

end Modules

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean

## Summary
- **Added (axiom-clean, 1):** `sheafifyTensorUnitIso` (private, line ~885) — the
  "sheafification is monoidal up to the unit" reconciliation brick. `#print axioms`
  = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (verified via `lean_verify`).
- **Blocked (3):** `pullbackTensorIso`, `pullbackUnitIso`, `IsInvertible.pullback` —
  all blocked on the SAME wall: `(Sheaf|Presheaf)OfModules.pullback` is an ABSTRACT
  left adjoint with no sectionwise/stalkwise formula in Mathlib. Left ABSENT (no
  sorry); precise pivot documented in-file (HANDOFF comment block after
  `sheafifyTensorUnitIso`).
- **Sorry count (whole file): 2 → 2** (the two pre-existing deferred dual-bridge
  sorries `exists_tensorObj_inverse` L693 and the PicSharp scaffold L1002 — untouched,
  per directive). No new sorry added. Full `lake env lean` compile clean.

## `sheafifyTensorUnitIso` (line ~885) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Statement:** for presheaves `P, Q` on `X`,
  `a(P ⊗ₚ Q) ≅ a((a P).val ⊗ₚ (a Q).val)` where `a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification
  (𝟙 𝒪_X)`, `⊗ₚ = PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf)`.
- **Approach:** the SAME technique that closes `tensorObj_assoc_iso` — whisker the
  sheafification unit `η` (a `J.W`-morphism via `W_toSheafify`) on each side
  (`η.app P ▷ Q`, then `(a P).val ◁ η.app Q`), both become `J.W` by the flatness-free
  `W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W` (Vestigial.lean), and `a` inverts `J.W`-maps by
  `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W`. Compose the two `asIso`s.
- **Gotchas:** (1) the STATEMENT must use `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj
  (R := X.presheaf)` explicitly (NOT `MonoidalCategory.tensorObj`), else the target's
  carrier `(a P).val : PresheafOfModules (Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf)` fails
  `MonoidalCategoryStruct` synthesis at elaboration time (mirrors how the project's
  own `tensorObj` is defined). (2) the left-whisker object must be given EXPLICITLY
  `(a.obj P).val ◁ η.app Q` — `_` can't be inferred. (3) keep the `letI instMS`
  bridge for the `Sheaf.val` carrier exactly as in the associator.
- **Why it matters:** this is the RHS reconciliation that the eventual
  `pullbackTensorIso` consumes — after `sheafificationCompPullback` + the presheaf
  pullback tensorator, the LHS lands at `a(PB(M.val) ⊗ₚ PB(N.val))`, while the RHS
  `tensorObj (f^*M)(f^*N) = a((f^*M).val ⊗ₚ (f^*N).val)` has each factor sheafified
  (via `pullbackIso`); `sheafifyTensorUnitIso` is exactly that bridge. It is a clean,
  reusable supplement valid even before the pullback comparison lands.

## `pullbackTensorIso` / `pullbackUnitIso` / `IsInvertible.pullback` — NOT ADDED (blocked)

### The wall (verified live this iter)
- `Scheme.Modules.pullback f = SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom`, and the
  underlying `PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom`, are BOTH `(pushforward _).leftAdjoint`
  — abstract left adjoints with **no sectionwise and no stalkwise formula** in Mathlib
  at the pinned commit (`grep` of `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/{Presheaf,Sheaf}/`
  finds no `pullback_obj` / `pullbackObjIso` / `pullback…Monoidal`).
- **The plan-agent recipe is structurally blocked**: "presheaf pullback is strong
  monoidal sectionwise via `(extendScalars f).Monoidal`; assemble the sectionwise
  tensorators `μ`" cannot typecheck — there is no sectionwise `(pullback φ).obj` to
  attach the `extendScalars` tensorator to. (`extendScalars` is strong monoidal in
  `ModuleCat`, but it is never definitionally the abstract presheaf pullback's value.)
- `Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal` gives the comparison MAP `δ : f^*(A⊗B) ⟶
  f^*A ⊗ f^*B` for free from `pushforward` lax-monoidal — but only at the PRESHEAF
  level (there is no `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules …)`). Proving `IsIso δ` still
  reduces (`isIso_of_isIso_app`) to a sectionwise identification of the abstract
  pullback with `extendScalars` = the same missing formula. Invertibility of `δ` is
  the genuine content (extend is strong, restrict only lax); not abstractly derivable.
- `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an iso only under `F.Final` (open
  immersions); false for general `f` (`PullbackFree.lean` L105).

### Probed (pullbackUnitIso, local route) — reduces to a Mathlib-absent naturality cluster
Drafted `pullbackUnitIso` via `isIso_of_isIso_restrict (pullbackObjUnitToUnit f) …`
(the local-chart-finality trick that proves `IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` for general
`f`, `LineBundlePullback.lean` L156). The per-chart goal (probed live) is
`IsIso ((restrictFunctor V.ι).map (pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom))`. Closing
it needs Mathlib-absent naturality of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` against `pullbackComp` /
`restrictFunctorIsoPullback` (so the restricted global map matches `pullbackObjUnitToUnit g`
for the local FINAL map `g = f.resLE U V`). A small lemma cluster, not a one-shot. The
draft (with its single probe `sorry`) was REMOVED to honor the no-sorry invariant.

### RECOMMENDED PIVOT for the planner (do NOT re-dispatch the sectionwise-`extendScalars` recipe)
1. **Local-chart-finality route** via the axiom-clean `isIso_of_isIso_restrict` (L567)
   + the local FINAL map `g = f.resLE U V` (`Opens.map g.base` final ⇒
   `pullbackObjUnitToUnit g` iso). First sub-step: the `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` /
   `pullbackComp` / `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` naturality cluster (Mathlib-absent,
   ~a handful of lemmas). For `pullbackTensorIso` this is harder — no canonical sheaf
   comparison map exists, so transport the presheaf oplax `δ` to the sheaf level (or
   build a stalkwise comparison) THEN apply the same local trick; `sheafifyTensorUnitIso`
   (landed) is the RHS reconciliation it will consume.
2. **STRATEGIC alternative** (cheapest): the RPF structure maps are the projection
   `π_T : C ×_S T → T` (FLAT, since `C → Spec k` is flat) and base changes thereof
   (also flat). A FLAT-restricted `IsInvertible.pullback`, or carrying pullback
   functoriality on the already-general `IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` and bridging to
   `IsInvertible` only at the group law, may avoid the general pullback-monoidal build
   entirely. Recommend a strategy-critic / mathlib-analogist pass on this BEFORE
   committing prover effort to route (1). NOTE: the `IsInvertible ⟺ IsLocallyTrivial`
   bridge's REVERSE direction (`IsLocallyTrivial ⟹ IsInvertible`) is the hard deferred
   dual-gluing, so "forward-then-pullback-then-reverse" is NOT a shortcut.

### Tooling note
Informal agent UNAVAILABLE this iter: `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` returns HTTP 401 (invalid
auth); `DEEPSEEK/OPENAI/OPENROUTER/GEMINI_API_KEY` unset. Could not obtain an external
sketch; the analysis above is Mathlib-source-derived.

## Why I stopped
**Partial progress.** 1 axiom-clean reusable declaration added (`sheafifyTensorUnitIso`,
line ~885), which is a genuine ingredient of the eventual `pullbackTensorIso`. The three
named targets are blocked on a precisely-identified Mathlib-API gap (abstract
left-adjoint pullback has no sectionwise/stalkwise formula), which makes the plan-agent's
sectionwise-`extendScalars` recipe structurally impossible. Per the progress-critic ts239
watch ("≥3 helpers without closing ⇒ consult mathlib-analogist, do NOT blind-redispatch"),
I added exactly ONE helper and STOPPED, handing off a concrete pivot (local-chart-finality
via `isIso_of_isIso_restrict`, or the FLAT-morphism strategic alternative) rather than
accumulating fragile scaffolding. No new sorry; file compiles clean.

## Blueprint markers
- `lem:sheafify…` — `sheafifyTensorUnitIso` is `private` and NOT `\lean{}`-pinned in
  the blueprint; no marker action needed.
- `lem:pullback_tensor_iso` / `lem:pullback_unit_iso` / `lem:isinvertible_pullback`
  (pins `pullbackTensorIso` / `pullbackUnitIso` / `IsInvertible.pullback`): these
  declarations do NOT exist yet — the blocks stay unmarked (correct).
